items.title: Man Utd latest: Ederson signs new Atalanta deal
England legend’s son, 18, quits Man City in shock transfer to club who narrowly avoided relegation last season
items.summary: REIGAN HESKEY has completed a shock transfer from Manchester City to FC Koln. The son of England legend Emile has made the move despite the club's struggles in the Bundesliga last season. Koln finished 14th in the German top-flight just three points above the relegation play-off spot. They have now

items.title: 'Police protection and threats' | Eckert reveals impact of Spygate scandal
items.summary: Speaking exclusively to Sky Sports' Mark McAdam, Southampton boss Tonda Eckert reveals the challenges he has faced after the Spygate scandal that resulted in their expulsion from the Championship play-offs.

items.title: Ederson extends Atalanta contract after Man Utd move collapses
items.summary: Atalanta midfielder Ederson extends his contract after a move to Manchester United collapses.

items.title: Arsenal want Villa's Rogers - Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Arsenal want Morgan Rogers, Mohamed Salah is set to join Besiktas, Manchester United are interested in Neco Williams, plus more.

items.title: Does Bryson improve his lie? DeChambeau given two-shot penalty after rules drama!
items.summary: Bryson DeChambeau was given a two-shot penalty after inadvertently improving his lie in thick rough on the fifth hole at The Open.

items.title: DeChambeau handed dramatic penalty in controversial Open ruling
items.summary: Bryson DeChambeau has been sensationally handed a two-shot penalty after being involved in a controversial rules breach during his second round at The Open.

items.title: Stick with Tuchel unless Guardiola is available - Rooney
items.summary: Wayne Rooney thinks the Football Association should stick with Thomas Tuchel as England head coach unless a move for Pep Guardiola is possible.

items.title: Why England’s third-place playoff vs France actually matters from Harry Kane honour to eerie good omen for 2028
items.summary: JUST imagine the ecstatic scenes at Birmingham Airport on Sunday. Thousands of fans on hand to greet a victorious England team after winning the World Cup third-place play-off. items.title: Saints boss Eckert reveals police protection amid threats during 'Spygate'
items.summary: Southampton boss Tonda Eckert has revealed he received police protection amid threats during the Spygate scandal.

items.title: Newcastle have £46m transfer bid rejected by Tottenham for Europa League winner who snubbed Barcelona but now wants out
items.summary: NEWCASTLE have had a £46million bid for Lucas Bergvall turned down by Spurs. It follows Nottingham Forest having a £38m offer for the Swede rejected earlier this month. Spurs have snubbed a number of offers for the 20-year-old from both sides this summer. But the North Londoners are open to a sale a

items.title: Some England players bemused by Tuchel subs in Argentina defeat
items.summary: A number of England players - including more than one senior player - were bemused by Thomas Tuchel's defensive substitutions in the World Cup semi-final against Argentina, Sky Sports News understands.

items.title: Tuchel urges England to finish strongly in emotional address
items.summary: England manager Thomas Tuchel has called on his players to end the World Cup strongly in an emotional address to the squad following their agonising semi-final defeat by Argentina.

items.title: Argentina players have free speech right to wave ‘Falklands are ours’ banner, Trump’s World Cup chief says
items.summary: ARGENTINA'S players had the right to wave the disgusting Falklands banner after their win against England, Donald Trump's World Cup chief has claimed. Lionel Messi and Co sparked fury for brandishing a vulgar sign saying “The Falklands are Argentinian” after their 2-1 semi-final victory. White House

items.title: Spain backed to beat ‘disgusting’ Argentina in World Cup final as England fans & celebs slam ‘classless’ Argies
items.summary: ENGLAND fans and showbiz stars lined up yesterday to roar a World Cup Final message: “Vamos España — Come on Spain!” They said Lionel Messi’s Argentina had got away with a masterclass in dirty tricks to beat the Three Lions 2-1 in the semi-final, then rounded it off by gleefully dancing around a sha

items.title: Will the World Cup final be canceled over wildfire smoke? We test air pollution at MetLife with game just hours away
items.summary: THE World Cup final is under a smoky cloud as the pollution outside MetLife Stadium has reached worrying levels for vulnerable fans. items.title: Embarrassed Fifa forced to slash ticket prices from £855 with thousands unsold for England’s World Cup clash with France
items.summary: FIFA are struggling to sell tickets for England’s third-place play-off with France. Around 7,000 tickets were still available as of 10am UK time on Friday which was 36 hours before kick-off in Miami. items.title: Fifa set to break OWN LAWS for second time at 2026 World Cup with glitzy half-time show chaos during final
items.summary: FIFA has calmed fears the World Cup Final will be badly disrupted by the planned half-time show - by insisting the total break will be just 17 minutes.
